spec_match.rationale: The simulator now models the Fetch Retry explainer's retryOptions shape, including maxAttempts as retries after the initial failure, retryAfterUnload, retryNonIdempotent, retryOnlyIfServerUnreached, maxAge, delay, and backoff.

interactive.rationale: Every retry policy knob updates the generated fetch snippet, explanatory notes, histogram, and delivery/drop statistics.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature index card by tuning retry policy settings and surfacing success-by-attempt, delivery timing, and maxAge drop behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now exposes non-idempotent method blocking, retryAfterUnload without keepalive, server-unreached-only retry, maxAge exhaustion, and non-retryable failures such as HTTP errors, aborts, CORS, CSP, and mixed content.

references.rationale: The see-also section cites chromestatus.com/feature/5181984581877760 and the Fetch Retry explainer.

design_system.rationale: The new controls and policy notes use shared CSS variables, explicit button colors, and readable chart label contrast.

summary: The major reference blocker and moderate spec-surface gaps are addressed. The script now parses, the API snippet uses the current retryOptions dictionary, and the simulator makes the important safety branches visible while noting that real fetch retry exposes only the final result to page script. Chrome DevTools MCP was not available in this session, so no browser-verification claim is made here.
